facet: patient-intake-matching — AI patient-intake + matching via the Claude Agent SDK
key_insight: The minimum patient information for early-phase eligibility is not a fixed intake form — it is computed backward from the candidate trials' parsed predicates. Intake should be a two-phase, minimum-necessary loop: (1) collect the small "early-phase eligibility kernel" (age/sex, primary diagnosis + histology, stage, prior lines of therapy, ECOG/performance status, the core organ-function labs [ANC, platelets, Hgb, creatinine/CrCl, bilirubin, AST/ALT], biomarker/variant status, major exclusion comorbidities, current meds, pregnancy/contraception) — just enough to seed recall-first retrieval; then (2) let the adjudication stage's `indeterminate — needs data` verdicts define, per candidate, the exact additional questions worth asking. This flips intake from "fill this big form" to "answer only what actually moves a live candidate from indeterminate to met/not-met," which is minimum-necessary (45 CFR 164.502(b)) made operational rather than asserted, and it is the same mechanism that already feeds the completeness ledger. The extraction agents never guess an absent datum — an unmappable field becomes `indeterminate`, preserving the recall-first / no-silent-not-met safety asymmetry end-to-end from intake through adjudication. The single highest-leverage new artifact is a triage/route-classifier agent that turns the structured profile + candidate set into a DRAFT rapid-access route recommendation (Mode A / Mode B / expanded access / no-option) with the hard-stop gates (insurance, IRB, billing, site-readiness) surfaced up front — this is the moment intake stops being data collection and starts making the physician able to act.

proposals.title: Predicate-driven minimum-necessary intake loop (the "smallest sufficient profile")
proposals.what_it_is: An intake orchestration that does not present a fixed form. It first collects the small early-phase eligibility kernel (age/sex, primary Dx + histology, stage, prior lines, ECOG, the core organ-function labs, biomarker/variant status, major exclusion comorbidities, current meds, pregnancy/contraception) to seed recall-first retrieval, then computes the additional intake questions as the UNION of `indeterminate — needs data` resolving questions across the current live candidate set. Intake terminates when no live candidate has an unresolved, patient-supplyable indeterminate criterion. Each answered item is the exact resolving question already emitted by adjudication (e.g. 'ECOG not in chart — required by inclusion #4').
proposals.value_to_whom: Physician (hcp-physician.md): collects the minimum that actually changes a candidate's status instead of a 100-field form, cutting intake time and respecting attention as the scarce resource. Patient: fewer, targeted questions. Compliance: minimum-necessary (164.502(b)) becomes verifiable from the audit trail — every asked datum traces to a specific parsed predicate, exactly as chart reads already do.
proposals.build_sketch: Reuse the parsed-predicate set from matching-eligibility-adjudication (EliIE/Criteria2Query-style parse → CQL/FHIR predicates). Build a gap agent that diffs (predicates referenced by live candidates) minus (profile fields present) → ordered question list, ranked by how many candidates each answer resolves. Loop: kernel → retrieve → adjudicate → surface resolving questions → ingest answers → re-adjudicate. Terminate on no-remaining-patient-supplyable-indeterminate. Every read/ask logged with the criterion index that justified it (NCT ID + criterion → query). Non-delegable respected: the loop only asks and drafts; it never coerces indeterminate → not-met and never returns an eligibility decision.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Checking/gap agent (deterministic diff over parsed predicates) + drafting agent to phrase the resolving questions in clinician- or patient-appropriate language; runs default-deny, read/draft tools only.

proposals.title: Multi-source extraction agent: chart snippet / free-text / dictation / pasted labs → provenance-tagged FHIR-shaped profile
proposals.what_it_is: A Claude SDK extraction agent that ingests heterogeneous inputs — a pasted chart snippet, free-text history, dictated summary, a photographed/PDF path or NGS report, copy-pasted lab panels — and emits a structured profile mapped to US Core / mCODE / Genomics Reporting profiles, with span-level provenance (source span → provenance → citation) and a per-field confidence. Anything it cannot map to a coded value with confidence is emitted as `indeterminate`, never guessed. This is the non-SMART on-ramp for the ~majority of solo/community clinicians whose usable data arrives as text, not a clean FHIR bundle.
proposals.value_to_whom: Physician without a research office (the load-bearing persona): can start matching from whatever they have — a dictation or a pasted note — without manual re-keying, which the SMART-on-FHIR page names as the error source it exists to avoid. Patient (self-intake path): can describe their situation in plain language and have it structured. Downstream: every extracted datum carries the provenance triple the generate/check/validate engine and Part 11 audit trail already require, so intake output is reproducible (input-hash) and auditable.
proposals.build_sketch: Extraction agent with output schema keyed to US Core resource types + mCODE oncology profiles + Genomics Reporting variant observations. Each extracted field carries {value, coded_concept (LOINC/SNOMED/RxNorm/HGVS), source_span, confidence, extraction_provenance}. Confidence below threshold or unmappable code → route to `indeterminate` bucket surfaced to the human for confirm/correct, never silently filled. Runs strictly inside the PREPARATORY privacy state (local, no PHI egress, ephemeral, audited reads); mirrors the read-mostly posture (never writes back to any EHR). Human review pass: extracted profile is a DRAFT the physician confirms field-by-field via single-pass-review-ux (deterministic vs inferred spans visually segregated) before it drives matching.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting/extraction agent (generate pass) with span-level provenance output contract; a separate review/QC agent flags low-confidence fields for human confirmation.

proposals.title: Rapid-access route classifier — DRAFT triage from profile + candidate set to Mode A / Mode B / expanded access / no-option
proposals.what_it_is: The bridge from matching to paperwork: a triage agent that takes the structured profile plus the adjudicated candidate ledger and drafts a rapid-access route recommendation across the three pathways — enroll in an existing trial (Mode A), stand up a sponsor-investigator n-of-1 (Mode B), pursue expanded access (312.310 / Form 3926), or a designed 'no suitable option' outcome — with the hard-stop gates (insurance/indemnification, no-IRB, Medicare billing compliance, physical site-readiness) surfaced UP FRONT, before any dossier drafting begins.
proposals.value_to_whom: Physician: this is the 'make me able to act' moment — instead of a ranked trial list, they get a recommended access route with the effort/cost estimate and the deal-breaker gates named at triage, exactly where hcp-physician.md says a physician who discovers the insurance gap after assembling the package 'has been failed by the tool.' Patient: the honest 'research is not treatment' / expanded-access-may-be-the-more-honest-frame distinction gets made structurally, mitigating therapeutic misconception. It is what turns matching into the entry point of the paperwork engine.
proposals.build_sketch: Deterministic decision logic over the ledger + profile + the site-readiness checklist (generated from the protocol's schedule of assessments): if a live Mode A trial exists and site can execute → Mode A; if intent is purely therapeutic and a manufacturer will supply → flag expanded access as the more honest frame; if a candidate mechanism-matched therapy has no open trial → Mode B with the full sponsor-floor burden surfaced. Output is a DRAFT recommendation with each gate as a green/amber/red completeness-ledger item, never an instruction to proceed. Non-delegable line held explicitly: it recommends and surfaces gates; the pathway decision, the dual-role/therapeutic-misconception judgment, and 'is Mode B ethical for this patient' stay with the physician and IRB.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Coordination + checking agent: mostly deterministic routing with a drafting agent to author the plain-language route rationale and the gate list; runs behind the gate-fails-closed permission posture.

proposals.title: Genomic/variant intake normalizer feeding the mechanism graph
proposals.what_it_is: A specialized extraction path for molecular data: paste or upload an NGS panel, molecular pathology report, or somatic/germline variant list, and the agent normalizes findings to HGNC gene + HGVS variant nomenclature, structures them as FHIR Genomics / mCODE observations, and grades therapeutic relevance against OncoKB (FDA-recognized levels) and CIViC — then hands them to the biomarker→pathway→target→trials mechanism-expansion graph.
proposals.value_to_whom: Physician + patient in precision-oncology cases: this is where the largest recall win lives — a colorectal patient with BRAF V600E surfaced into a BRAF-mutant basket trial not indexed under 'colorectal,' the exact failure a condition-string matcher cannot fix. Turns an unstructured NGS PDF (how variants actually arrive at a community clinic) into the structured molecular input the mechanism graph needs. Evidence tiers (OncoKB level) are surfaced, not overridden, keeping expansion inside the retrieval COU.
proposals.build_sketch: Extraction agent → HGNC/HGVS normalization → FHIR Genomics + mCODE variant observations with source-span provenance. Enrich each variant with OncoKB/CIViC evidence level as a labeled edge; emit low-confidence/ambiguous variant calls as indeterminate for human confirm. Feed normalized variants into the existing matching-mechanism-graph retrieval stage; the biological-link strength (direct target vs downstream-pathway vs class effect) is labeled so the clinician weights it. Non-delegable: interpreting the variant for therapeutic action is the practice of medicine (molecular-tumor-board judgment) — the agent surfaces cited candidates only, never asserts actionability.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Extraction/normalization agent + knowledge-base matching agent (OncoKB/CIViC lookup); provenance-carrying, runs in PREPARATORY state.

proposals.title: Dual front-door intake with role-scoped privacy (physician SMART-launch vs patient self-intake)
proposals.what_it_is: Two intake entry points sharing one extraction/matching core but with different privacy egress rules. Physician path: SMART App Launch (point of care) or Backend Services bulk $export (panel screen) — reads the certified (g)(10) API directly, minimum-necessary read scopes, no PHI egress. Patient self-intake path: a plain-language intake that produces a profile which either (a) stays local as a de-identified seed the patient physically carries to a clinician, or (b) triggers a physician-invite handoff — but never emits raw match output to the patient, honoring the patient.md gate that matching output goes to the HCP.
proposals.value_to_whom: Patient in an under-resourced setting whose real barrier is 'absence of a physician with a research office' (patient.md): gives them a way to start and a stated recourse, without exposing their record or inviting self-triage/therapeutic misconception. Physician: receives a pre-structured seed profile and a clean SMART-launch path. Compliance: keeps every path inside the privacy-state-machine's PREPARATORY boundary; the patient-facing status copy ('your doctor received N candidate programs on [date]', expected-response window) is treated as IRB-reviewable recruitment-adjacent material, per the patient.md minimum-transparency floor.
proposals.build_sketch: Router in front of the shared extraction core. Physician path wires the two SMART-on-FHIR acquisition modes (App Launch OAuth2+PKCE / Backend Services signed-JWT, read-only .rs scopes). Patient path runs the free-text extraction agent locally, produces a de-identified seed (164.514(b) safe-harbor field stripping) and a physician-invite artifact; raw candidate list is withheld from the patient portal by design, replaced with the transparency-floor status view and stated recourse (second opinion / self-search ClinicalTrials.gov / self-referral). No PHI leaves the covered-entity boundary before an authorized transition. Non-delegable/consent gates untouched — self-intake never enrolls anyone.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Shared extraction agent + coordination agents for role-scoped routing and the physician-invite handoff; permission modes enforce the patient-path egress restriction in code.

proposals.title: Confirmed-profile handoff: intake output pre-populates screening/eligibility worksheets as provenance-cited drafts
proposals.what_it_is: The closing loop from intake into the generate/check/validate engine. Once the physician confirms the structured profile and adjudicates candidates, every confirmed `met` criterion and its patient-datum citation pre-populates the screening/eligibility worksheet and downstream activation documents as DRAFTS; every unresolved `indeterminate` becomes an amber open-item on the completeness ledger with its exact resolving question as the ledger entry. The intake profile itself becomes the hashed structured input the Part 11 audit trail references for reproducibility.
proposals.value_to_whom: Physician: intake data is entered once and flows into the paperwork — no re-keying the same diagnosis/labs into the eligibility worksheet, the 1572 support, the protocol synopsis. Compliance: the input-hash makes every generated draft reproducible and tamper-evident (Part 11 / ALCOA++), and the amber ledger items make the remaining gaps explicit rather than silent. This is what makes 'genuinely useful matching' continuous with 'complete paperwork built rapidly.'
proposals.build_sketch: On profile confirmation, hash the structured profile as the generation input-of-record. Map confirmed met-criteria + datum citations to worksheet fields via the existing draft-provenance-model triple. Indeterminate verdicts → completeness-ledger amber entries carrying the resolving question. Every span in the resulting drafts is span-cited to the intake datum. Gates preserved: documents advance only to `checked` by agents; the `human-reviewed → signed` transition stays with an authenticated human e-signature; nothing auto-files.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agents (template instantiation from the confirmed profile) + review/QC agent for the completeness check against the essential-records matrix; deterministic validate pass unchanged.

proposals.title: Route-3926 Sprint Pack: same-day single-patient expanded-access package
proposals.what_it_is: A one-sitting pipeline that takes a structured physician intake (patient clinical picture, drug, manufacturer, IRB of record) and emits the COMPLETE Form 3926 package: filled 3926 with the 10.a/10.b waiver logic applied, clinical history + treatment plan narrative, manufacturer LOA request letter, IRB chair-concurrence submission, drug-accountability record shell, and the 50.25-compliant ICF — each artifact carrying provenance triples and the package shipping with its completeness ledger as the cover sheet. An emergency mode variant starts the 312.310(d) 15-working-day written-follow-up clock and the 56.104(c) 5-working-day IRB-notification clock at telephone authorization.
proposals.value_to_whom: The treating physician with no research office — the guiding-scenario persona. Today an expanded-access attempt dies on paperwork friction; this makes the fastest lawful route to a named patient's drug an afternoon's work instead of a month's. Also the patient: time-to-drug is the product metric.
proposals.build_sketch: Extend the existing engine registry with a 3926 pathway profile: the requirements universe is small and fixed (3926 fields per FDA instructions, 312.305(a)/312.310(a) criteria coverage, LOA-reference-or-FDA-division-contact rule, consent-before-treatment gate, ClinicalTrials.gov suppression per 42 CFR 11.10). Claude SDK drafting subagents fill narrative sections (clinical history, treatment plan, risk rationale) strictly from intake data; deterministic code fills form fields and computes the emergency clocks. Gates stay intact: physician signature + certifications, the 312.310(a) risk determination, manufacturer LOA (documented external event), IRB concurrence, and the consent event are all ambers that only humans discharge. Ship as the first end-to-end vertical because the wiki itself calls 3926 'among the most tractable generate/check/validate targets in the entire system.'
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agents for narrative sections (clinical history, treatment plan, §312.305 criteria rationale, LOA request letter in professional regulatory register); an extraction agent that maps free-text physician intake onto the structured StudyFacts record with per-field confidence, routing low-confidence extractions to red.

proposals.title: Fact Spine + parallel drafting swarm: consistency by construction, not by checking
proposals.what_it_is: A single canonical StudyFacts record (PI identity, IND number, protocol version/date, drug, dose/route/duration, site, IRB, sub-investigators) that is the ONLY permitted source for any fact appearing in any document. N drafting agents run in parallel — one per document in the route's registry set — each contractually bound to (a) never assert a fact absent from the spine (missing data becomes [[MISSING]] → red, never an invention) and (b) emit a provenance triple per span. Because every document transcribes the same spine, cross-document consistency (the classic multi-document failure mode) holds by construction; the deterministic consistency checker becomes a verification of the invariant rather than the only defense. This is the mechanism that makes 'obscene amounts of paperwork done correctly' scale: 6 documents or 60, the marginal correctness cost is near zero.
proposals.value_to_whom: All three routes and all personas. The sponsor-investigator (Route 3) gets a full 312.23 IND package (1571, investigational plan, protocol, 1572, 3674, financial disclosure) drafted in parallel in minutes; the pharma counterparty (Route 2) gets a site dossier where PI name, license, and delegation log agree everywhere — the exact trust signal a skeptical sponsor checks first.
proposals.build_sketch: Formalize StudyFacts as the engine's data-model entity with typed fields keyed to the 60-doc registry's required_fields. Claude Agent SDK orchestrator fans out one drafting subagent per registry document (bounded context: template + spine + relevant reference-doc excerpts only — no shared chat state between drafters). Post-generation, the existing check.py consistency pass runs as the invariant verifier; any span whose provenance doesn't trace to the spine or a template is auto-marked inferred and foregrounded in review. Human-in-the-loop unchanged: parallel drafting produces drafts faster; nothing exits except through the sign-off gates.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Parallel drafting subagents (one per document) via SDK sub-agent dispatch; an extraction agent that populates the spine from intake documents (CV, license, prior 1572, manufacturer package insert) with human confirmation of each extracted fact before it enters the spine.

proposals.title: Adversarial QC lane with regulatory-register lint (the anti-slop gate)
proposals.what_it_is: A structurally separate checking layer that every package passes before any human sees it: (1) an independent Claude QC agent with zero shared context with the drafters, red-teaming for internal contradictions, unsupported spans, stale versions, and missed conditional requirements; (2) a deterministic register linter that enforces the professional-voice requirement — bans LLM mannerisms ('It's not just X but Y', 'It's worth noting', 'delve', 'crucial', em-dash chains, hedged filler), bans superlatives and unqualified benefit claims per the ICF §7 calibration rules ('promising', 'breakthrough', 'will improve'), flags benefits-section length disproportionate to risks, and scores narrative sections against a style profile built from a corpus of real filed 3926s/INDs/protocols. Register-lint failures block the check pass exactly like a missing field does. The human reviewer receives draft + machine critique together, so review starts where the adversary found weakness.
proposals.value_to_whom: The physician (whose professional name is on every page — an AI-mannered filing damages their credibility with FDA and sponsors), the pharma counterparty (an obviously-LLM dossier is an instant rejection signal), and OSSICRO itself (the vision names slop-free regulatory register as non-negotiable; this makes it a machine-enforced contract instead of an aspiration).
proposals.build_sketch: Two components. Deterministic: a lint rule-pack in validate.py (pattern lists + section-ratio checks + FK-grade readability where applicable), versioned and citation-mapped like every other rule so it ships with authority traces. Agentic: an SDK checker agent invoked with ONLY the assembled package and the requirements matrix — never the drafting transcripts — whose findings are appended to the ledger as reds (defect: fix required) or annotations on ambers (reviewer attention pointers). The QC agent cannot edit documents; it can only file findings, preserving the drafter/checker structural separation the wiki already specifies. Corpus style profile built offline from OSSICRO's source library of real FDA-filed documents.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Independent checking agent (fresh context, adversarial system prompt); a rewrite agent that, on lint failure, proposes a register-compliant revision routed back through generate→check rather than patched in place.

proposals.title: Red-Resolver: ledger-driven intake interview that converges to zero reds
proposals.what_it_is: The completeness ledger already states, for every red, THE exact question whose answer resolves it. This proposal closes the loop: an intake agent walks the physician (or patient, for patient-side facts) through precisely those questions — and only those — in dependency order, recomputing the ledger after every answer, until reds hit zero. Where a red names a chart datum, it becomes a SMART-on-FHIR fetch request (through the privacy state machine) instead of a question; where it names a counterparty artifact (manufacturer LOA, IRB letter), it becomes a tracked outbound request with a drafted letter attached. The session's definition of done is a computed state — ledger: 0 red, all amber mapped to named humans — not a feeling of completeness.
proposals.value_to_whom: The physician: instead of confronting 60 templates, they answer a bounded, shrinking question list on their phone between patients. The micro-CRO: every open item is either a machine task or a named human's task, so nothing rots silently. This is what makes 'very quickly' true in practice — elapsed time collapses to the sum of unanswered questions plus counterparty latency.
proposals.build_sketch: Thin loop over the existing engine: run check.py → take reds → order by dependency (spine facts before documents that consume them) → SDK interview agent asks in plain clinical language, writes answers into StudyFacts (each write provenance-stamped 'physician-attested, date, session') → regenerate affected documents → recompute ledger → repeat. Ledger discipline is untouched: the agent supplies data that makes predicates pass; it never grades, never dispositions a red (not-applicable calls are a human's audited act), never touches amber. Frontend is a single progress surface: red count falling, amber list forming into the signature-routing queue.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Interview agent (question phrasing, answer normalization into typed fields, clarifying follow-ups when an answer is ambiguous); matching-adjacent extraction agent for chart-derived answers when a FHIR document lands.

proposals.title: Sign-and-Ship Cockpit: verifiable package assembly and single-pass gate discharge
proposals.what_it_is: The terminal 'assemble' stage the pipeline currently implies but doesn't ship: takes a ledger-green/amber package and produces (1) a hash-chained package manifest (every artifact, version, provenance index, ledger snapshot — the verifiable-dossier property, independently checkable by an IRB or pharma counterparty); (2) the single-pass review surface — deterministic and boilerplate spans collapsed with provenance on demand, inferred/interpretive spans foregrounded, the amber list rendered as an ordered signature-routing queue naming each act, its human, its authority, and its evidence-of-discharge; (3) Part 11 sign-off capture (§§11.50–11.300) that discharges ambers one by one and flips document states; (4) final export — filled 3926/1571/1572 PDFs on the official forms, IRB submission bundle, manufacturer request packet — unlocked only when the state machine permits, with submission itself remaining an explicit human-authorized act.
proposals.value_to_whom: The physician-reviewer: one sitting, attention pre-triaged to the judgment calls, no 200-page rubber-stamp trap — this is what makes the gates real protection rather than ceremony. The IRB and pharma counterparties: they receive a package whose completeness claim is independently verifiable (requirement → artifact → citation → responsible-human signature), which is OSSICRO's Route 2 acceptance wedge.
proposals.build_sketch: Build on models.py's document state machine and gates.py: add a PackageManifest object (content hashes, ledger snapshot hash-chain per 11.10(e)), a review-UI that renders the ledger as the cover sheet and provenance triples as expandable annotations, and an e-signature ceremony that calls gates.record_signoff with authenticated identity. Official-form PDF fill is deterministic code (AcroForm field mapping for 3926/1571/1572 — schemas are fixed and published). No SDK agent touches this stage except an optional summarizer that drafts the reviewer's plain-language cover memo — itself provenance-bound and lint-checked. GateViolation remains the enforcement: export before discharge raises, correctly.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Minimal by design — a cover-memo drafting agent only; assembly, hashing, state transitions, and signature capture are deterministic software validated under Part 11 §11.10(a), per the engine's own credibility tiering.

proposals.title: Counterparty Hooks Pack: the outbound artifacts that pull pharma and CRO layers toward yes
proposals.what_it_is: A generation profile for the documents whose AUDIENCE is the counterparty rather than FDA: the manufacturer LOA request (with the §561A expanded-access-policy citation for that specific company pre-researched and quoted), the Route 2 verifiable site-qualification dossier (1572 + CVs + licenses + 3454/3455 + GCP certs + delegation log + regulatory-binder index, ledger attached, hash-verifiable), the safety-data-exchange agreement draft, and the ToRO draft that transfers a named excess obligation to the micro-CRO accountable layer. Each ships with a counterparty-gate tracker: manufacturer LOA, sponsor site acceptance, and IRB letter are ambers of the documented-external-event kind, with drafted follow-up correspondence queued (never auto-sent) at staleness thresholds.
proposals.value_to_whom: The physician facing the two consents you cannot buy — manufacturer supply and sponsor acceptance. OSSICRO can't compel either, but it can make the request maximally answerable: a complete, internally consistent, citation-carrying dossier answers the skeptical sponsor's trust question before it's asked. This is the 'hooks and access into the CRO layer and the pharmaceutical layer' clause of the vision made concrete: hooks are artifacts counterparties can verify and act on, plus tracked request/response state.
proposals.build_sketch: Add counterparty-facing templates to the registry with an audience field driving the register linter's style profile (persuasive-professional to a pharma medical-affairs reader vs. neutral-regulatory to FDA). SDK research agent compiles the target manufacturer's posted §561A expanded-access policy and IIS program contact path into the request draft, with every claim cited to the company's own posted policy. Counterparty gates are ledger ambers discharged only by the documented external event (LOA received and filed, acceptance letter, IRB letter); follow-up drafts present for review — no autonomous send, ever. Route 2's dossier reuses the Fact Spine so it is consistent with the FDA-facing set by construction.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Research agent (locate and quote the manufacturer's posted expanded-access policy / IIS portal); drafting agent for LOA request and follow-up correspondence in counterparty register; extraction agent that parses a received LOA or acceptance letter into the evidence record a human then confirms.

proposals.title: Golden-Package Harness: regression evals that keep correctness true at obscene volume
proposals.what_it_is: An evaluation harness of synthetic study fixtures (expanded from the existing sample_study.json: emergency 3926, non-emergency 3926, Route 2 site dossier, Route 3 full IND, pediatric-assent ICF, non-English short-form set, no-LOA-possible branch) each paired with a human-verified gold package. Every change to a template, rule, prompt, or model version replays the full fixture set and reports: form-field exactness, provenance coverage (percent of spans with valid triples), ledger-grade agreement with gold, register-lint pass rate, and — critically — zero gate regressions (no fixture ever reaches final without its sign-offs; GateViolation tests stay red-line). This is the engine's own credibility file under the FDA 2025 AI draft guidance: a measured, versioned record that the drafting COU performs as claimed.
proposals.value_to_whom: OSSICRO's maintainers and, downstream, every physician whose filing depends on the engine not having silently regressed when a prompt or model changed. It is also the artifact that lets OSSICRO defend the low-influence/low-consequence COU classification with data rather than assertion — the difference between an interpretive position and a supported one when a sponsor's QA or an FDA reviewer asks how the AI is controlled.
proposals.build_sketch: Extend engine/tests: fixtures directory grows one JSON study + one gold package per scenario; a replay runner executes generate→check→validate→assemble headlessly and diffs against gold with typed comparators (exact for form fields and ledger grades, semantic-similarity threshold plus mandatory-element checklist for narrative sections, scored by an SDK judge agent whose own rubric is versioned). CI-gate merges on it. Model/prompt version is recorded in every run per the Part 11 attribution requirement already specified in the wiki. No new human-loop implications — this is infrastructure that protects the existing gates.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: LLM-judge agent for narrative-section fidelity scoring (element coverage, calibration, register) with a frozen, versioned rubric; optionally a fixture-synthesis agent to draft new scenario fixtures for human curation.

key_insight: Correctness at 'obscene volume' cannot come from checking harder after the fact — it comes from making error structurally impossible upstream: one canonical fact spine that every parallel drafting agent transcribes (so cross-document consistency holds by construction), provenance triples that make invention detectable per span, a register linter that makes slop a blocking defect equal to a missing field, and the completeness ledger as the computed definition of done (zero reds, every amber a named human's act). The Claude SDK's role is deliberately confined — drafting, extraction, adversarial critique, judging — inside deterministic rails (form fill, consistency, clocks, gates, Part 11 signatures) so that speed scales with agent parallelism while the eight non-delegable gates never move: the physician's day compresses from months of paperwork to answering the ledger's open questions and one triaged review-and-sign sitting, with time-to-drug then bounded only by the counterparties the law says must decide.

proposals.title: Say-Yes Package: route-specific, sponsor-native export packs
proposals.what_it_is: A one-click export layer that renders a physician's rapid-access package in exactly the shape the receiving pharma function already consumes. Three variants keyed to the route triage: (R1) LOA-request pack for expanded access — Form 3926 draft + clinical narrative + treatment/monitoring plan + physician qualification statement + consent form + IRB/56.105 path + a signature-ready LOA skeleton for the manufacturer; (R3) IIS pack — concept synopsis + protocol draft + qualification dossier + FMV budget + support-agreement and SDEA scaffolds shaped for a Medical Affairs review committee; (R2) full activation binder in TMF Reference Model naming/indexing with the expected counter-offers (nearest-site referral, R1 conversion) pre-briefed. Every pack ships with a professional cover letter, a human-readable cross-reference index (requirement → artifact → citation → signer), visible-signature PDF renditions, and the hash manifest + written verification procedure as an appendix nobody is required to run.
proposals.value_to_whom: Pharma (Medical Affairs / expanded-access coordinators / Clinical Development): a request that arrives complete, internally consistent, in their own document conventions, verifiable in a single checklist pass — collapsing the six-figure onboarding-machinery problem for a one-patient engagement. Physician: the deliverable that actually gets a yes. Micro-CRO: its tangible sellable product.
proposals.build_sketch: Extend the existing generate/check/validate engine with per-route export profiles (a registry mapping the 60-doc set to R1/R2/R3 minimal-but-compliant subsets from the wiki's acceptance-gate list). Claude Agent SDK drafting agent renders the cover letter and cross-reference index in regulatory register; a separate checking agent enforces cross-document consistency (names, dates, license numbers, versions agree) and runs the de-slop/voice pass against templates; the completeness ledger blocks export while any gate item (signature, IRB approval, physician determination) is amber/red — export is only of drafts-for-review or human-executed artifacts. No document leaves without the physician's explicit release action.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agent (cover letter, cross-reference index, narrative) + checking agent (cross-document consistency, register/anti-slop validation) + a completeness-verifier agent that maps each route's acceptance-gate items 1-8 to concrete artifacts before export unlocks.

proposals.title: Manufacturer Front-Door Registry + Route Router
proposals.what_it_is: A maintained, machine-readable directory of every manufacturer's sanctioned intake points — the §561A-mandated published expanded-access policies, IIS/grants portals (Genentech, Roche, LEO, Acadia pattern), and medical-information contacts — normalized into structured records: which function is the counterparty, required intake fields, submission format, drug-specific programs, stated decision timelines. The router takes the patient/drug facts, applies the wiki's route-likelihood ordering (R1 > R3 > R2), resolves the drug to its manufacturer's actual front door, and pre-maps the Say-Yes Package fields onto that company's specific intake form. Hard-coded rail: routes only to Medical Affairs or Clinical Development site-selection — a sales contact is a validation error, never a fallback.
proposals.value_to_whom: Physician: eliminates the weeks lost finding the right door and being bounced between functions — the single biggest non-regulatory delay in rapid access. Pharma: requests land at the sanctioned function, in their format, pre-triaged to the route they are most likely to grant — which is precisely what makes fast yeses possible and protects their compliance firewall.
proposals.build_sketch: Claude Agent SDK extraction agent crawls published expanded-access policies and IIS portal pages into a versioned YAML/JSON registry (source URL + retrieval date + hash per record); a freshness agent re-checks entries on a schedule and diffs; every registry entry that will drive an actual submission is human-confirmed before first use (a stale contact wastes the patient's clock). Router is deterministic code over the registry + the engine's triage output; the physician sees and approves the chosen route and recipient before anything is addressed. The registry itself is an open-source asset — arguably the highest-leverage public good in the whole layer.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Extraction agent (policy/portal parsing into structured records) + freshness/diff checking agent; the routing decision surface is deterministic and human-approved.

proposals.title: Hooks-as-Contracts Kit: LOA, SDEA, and TORO instrument builders
proposals.what_it_is: The three signable instruments that legally constitute the CRO/pharma connection, built as first-class engine documents rather than afterthoughts. (1) LOA skeleton: the §312.305(b)(1) cross-reference letter drafted for the manufacturer's counsel to edit and sign — minimizing their drafting cost to near zero. (2) SDEA scaffold: bidirectional ICSR flow with the 312.32 7/15-day clocks reconciled against contractual reporting to the manufacturer's pharmacovigilance, wired to the safety-clock engine. (3) TORO builder: an interactive instrument that forces obligation-by-obligation enumeration across the full 312.50-312.59 transferable set — every obligation explicitly marked TRANSFERRED or RETAINED (encoding 312.52's silence-means-retained rule as a completeness check), each transferred item bound to a named qualified human at the micro-CRO, and non-transferable investigator duties (312.60-312.69) structurally excluded from the picker.
proposals.value_to_whom: Pharma counsel: a supply decision becomes an edit-and-sign, not a drafting project. Micro-CRO: TOROs that are inspection-clean by construction, with 312.52(b) enforcement exposure scoped precisely. Physician: the safety and supply plumbing exists on day one instead of being discovered at the first SAE.
proposals.build_sketch: Three new templates in the registry with a TORO-specific validation rule: check pass fails if any 312.50-312.59 obligation lacks an explicit transferred/retained mark, if a transferred obligation lacks a named qualified human, or if any 312.60-312.69 item appears. Claude SDK drafting agent fills instruments from study facts; checking agent verifies CFR-anchor completeness and that no non-delegable function (causality adjudication, consent, IRB judgment, signatures) is enumerated as transferred to software or to an unstaffed role. All three instruments are drafts-for-counsel — signatures are the manufacturer's, sponsor-investigator's, and micro-CRO principals' alone.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agent (instrument population from study facts) + checking agent (enumeration completeness, CFR anchoring, non-delegable exclusion enforcement).

proposals.title: Checklist Join: sponsor-questionnaire ingestion and mapping agent
proposals.what_it_is: Pharma will never adopt OSSICRO's format; OSSICRO must adopt theirs. This tool ingests the sponsor's own artifact — feasibility questionnaire, site-qualification checklist, IIS application form, expanded-access intake form (PDF/DOCX/XLSX/portal fields) — and generates the join against the dossier: each sponsor line-item mapped to the specific OSSICRO artifact, governing citation, and signer, with unmet items rendered as an explicit gap list carrying the exact resolving question. Output is the cross-reference index in the sponsor's own ordering and vocabulary, so their reviewer performs a checklist join inside their ordinary workflow — no OSSICRO software, no new format, no transformation.
proposals.value_to_whom: Pharma reviewer: single-pass verification against their own checklist — the operational definition of 'trivial to say yes.' Physician/micro-CRO: responds to any sponsor's idiosyncratic diligence format in hours; the no-track-record site presents better-organized evidence than experienced sites. Over time, accumulated mappings become a library of major sponsors' gates.
proposals.build_sketch: Claude Agent SDK matching agent parses the sponsor document into line-items and proposes mappings into the 60-doc registry / manifest tuples with per-mapping confidence and citation; a human (physician or micro-CRO reviewer) confirms or corrects every mapping before the index is generated — a wrong mapping in a diligence response is a credibility-destroying event, so the human confirm step is non-negotiable. Confirmed mappings persist as reusable per-sponsor profiles feeding the Say-Yes Package export. Renderer is deterministic from confirmed mappings.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Matching agent (sponsor line-item → registry artifact, with confidence + citation) and extraction agent (parsing heterogeneous sponsor formats); human confirms all mappings.

proposals.title: Micro-CRO Workbench: the accountable layer's operating console
proposals.what_it_is: What the micro-CRO plugs into. A review-queue and obligation-tracking console scoped by TORO: (1) Tier-2 gate queue — packages awaiting qualified-human review at accountable gates, each with the checking agent's pre-review findings attached (flagged inconsistencies, unmet criteria, register violations) so human review time goes to judgment, not hunting; (2) TORO-scoped obligation board — per-study view of exactly which 312.50-312.59 obligations this entity has assumed, each bound to its named human, with safety-clock escalations routed to whoever holds 312.32 operations; (3) E6(R3) oversight-evidence log — an append-only record of every human review act, decision, and signer, which doubles as the entity's BIMO inspection-readiness file and its accumulating performance record (the instrument for winning the second trial, per the dossier page).
proposals.value_to_whom: Micro-CRO: a thin entity with 312.52(b) enforcement parity can only stay thin if its per-obligation overhead is near zero — this console is what makes the thin-entity model economically viable. Sponsor-investigator: visible confirmation of what the entity holds vs. what remains theirs. Pharma vendor-qualification auditors: an inspectable oversight trail on request.
proposals.build_sketch: Web console over the existing engine state (completeness ledger, document state machine, provenance model) plus the TORO builder's structured output as the scoping datum. Claude SDK checking agents run pre-review on every queued document and draft the review-finding summary; the console records only human dispositions — approve, reject, escalate — under Part 11 signature discipline. No agent can advance a document past a Tier-2 gate; the queue is structurally a human bottleneck, by design. Safety-clock escalation is deterministic code (deadline computation), with the causality call itself always presented to the qualified physician.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Checking agents (pre-review findings, consistency flags, summary drafts for the human reviewer); zero agent authority over gate disposition.

proposals.title: Safety Exchange Conduit: E2B(R3)-shaped ICSR drafts for manufacturer PV intake
proposals.what_it_is: The ongoing hook after the yes. Adverse events intaken on the physician side are coded (draft MedDRA terms), narrated, and rendered simultaneously as (a) a MedWatch 3500A draft for the sponsor-investigator's own 312.32 filing and (b) an E2B(R3)-conformant ICSR draft that the manufacturer's safety database (Argus, ArisGlobal) can ingest without transformation — discharging the SDEA's contractual reporting obligation in the format pharma's pharmacovigilance machinery natively expects. Both clocks (FDA 7/15-day; SDEA contractual) are computed and escalated by the safety-clock engine.
proposals.value_to_whom: Pharma pharmacovigilance: safety data from n-of-1 sites arrives machine-ingestible instead of as faxed narratives — removes a standing reason to refuse small sites, since PV intake burden is a real line-item in the supply decision. Physician: the heaviest recurring post-authorization obligation becomes draft-review instead of form-assembly. Patient: the safety spine that keeps supply flowing.
proposals.build_sketch: E2B(R3) XML renderer as a deterministic serializer over the engine's structured AE record; Claude SDK extraction agent structures the clinical event from source notes and drafts the narrative + candidate MedDRA coding; checking agent validates the XML against the E2B schema and cross-checks narrative/coded-field consistency. Hard gates preserved end-to-end: causality and expectedness determinations are presented to and made by the qualified physician; nothing transmits to FDA or to the manufacturer without the physician's release. Build after the SDEA kit — the conduit implements what that instrument promises.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Extraction agent (event structuring, narrative draft, candidate coding) + checking agent (schema validation, narrative/field consistency); causality and filing are exclusively human.

key_insight: The 'API into pharma' is not an API. Sponsors will never grant write access to validated CTMS/EDC systems, and their reviewers will never execute third-party software — so the integration surface is necessarily document-shaped and contract-shaped: packages rendered in the recipient's own conventions and checklists (TMF Reference Model naming, their questionnaire, their intake form), joined to three signable instruments — LOA, SDEA, TORO — that legally constitute the connection. Standardize the package, not the pipe: make the manufacturer's yes an edit-and-sign, the reviewer's verification a single checklist join in their ordinary workflow, and the micro-CRO's assumed obligations an enumerated, named-human-staffed board. Everything AI does is drafting, extraction, matching, and checking behind human release gates; the hooks that move drug, data, and money are all human signatures by design.

proposals.title: Fill-only-the-blanks generation contract (template-anchored drafting, structurally enforced)
proposals.what_it_is: A hard engine invariant: the drafting agent never emits document prose — it emits only typed field values for the placeholders enumerated in each template's Fields table (which already maps placeholder → study-record path → citation). The engine performs the interpolation into the vetted template. Enforcement is a deterministic diff: rendered document minus template must reduce to placeholder substitutions only; any other byte delta fails generation. Verbatim-required passages (the 21 CFR 50.25(c) ClinicalTrials.gov statement, the 1572 item 9 commitments block) are SHA-256-locked in registry entries and hard-fail on any change.
proposals.value_to_whom: Physician (sponsor-investigator): the surrounding regulatory prose was written and vetted by humans against cited authority, so document voice is a structural property, not a behavioral hope — slop cannot appear in the frame at all. Patient: consent boilerplate that IRBs recognize stays intact. Micro-CRO/IRB reviewers: review burden collapses to field values plus a redline, because the template is invariant.
proposals.build_sketch: Extend engine/ossicro/generate.py: (1) parse each template in templates/ into an AST of {fixed-prose spans, placeholder slots, [INSTRUCTION] blocks}; (2) drafting agent (Claude SDK, model claude-opus-4-8, structured outputs via messages.parse with a Pydantic model generated per-template from the Fields table, strict:true so field types validate exactly) returns only field values; (3) engine interpolates and runs the template-diff invariant; (4) add verbatim_hashes to registry/documents.json for every passage marked 'required verbatim' / 'do not alter' and check them in check.py. Instruction blocks are stripped mechanically at finalize, never by the model. Human gate unchanged — output is still DRAFT for qualified review; this narrows what the human must scrutinize to the filled fields.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agent constrained to structured field-value output (messages.parse + per-template Pydantic schema, strict tool use); the anti-slop guarantee itself is deterministic engine code, not the model

proposals.title: Deterministic register linter with a versioned banned-construction registry
proposals.what_it_is: A zero-LLM lint pass in check.py driven by registry/style-rules.json: banned constructions ('it is important to note', 'it's worth noting', 'not just X, but Y', 'plays a crucial/vital role', 'delve', 'seamless', 'robust' as filler, 'in today's landscape', hollow closers like 'in conclusion'), tic metrics (em-dash density per 1,000 words, sentence-initial 'Additionally/Moreover' rate, hedging stacks like 'may potentially'), promotional-adjective list, and per-register rules keyed to document category: consent fields must be second person and non-exculpatory; FDA-form worksheets must be third person, no first-person editorializing. Each rule carries severity, a confirmed/interpretive classification (matching the wiki's calibration standard), and a citation where one exists (e.g., exculpatory-language phrases → 21 CFR 50.20).
proposals.value_to_whom: All personas, cheapest layer first: physicians get documents that read as work product an IRB or pharma partner takes seriously on first contact; the project gets an auditable, diffable definition of 'slop' that doesn't depend on LLM judgment and runs in milliseconds on every generation. Pharma/CRO hooks: partners can inspect the rule registry itself as evidence of QC.
proposals.build_sketch: New module engine/ossicro/style.py + registry/style-rules.json (rule id, pattern/metric, scope: field-value|full-document|category, severity, citation, status confirmed|interpretive). Wire into the existing check pass so a style failure is a check finding like any completeness finding, recorded in the ledger with provenance. Rules resting on interpretive positions fail toward the human gate, never auto-pass — same principle the wiki already states for validation rules. No LLM anywhere in this layer; it is the fast pre-filter before the voice agent runs.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: n/a (deliberately deterministic; it gates what the SDK agents produced)

proposals.title: Voice-validation checking agent with per-category regulatory-register rubrics
proposals.what_it_is: A Claude SDK checking agent that judges only the filled field values (short spans, thanks to proposal 1) against an explicit rubric per document category: consent register (plain language, second person, risks stated without minimization, no promotional framing of benefits, payment never listed as benefit), FDA-form register (factual, transcribable, no narrative), protocol/M11 register (precise, cited, non-promotional), correspondence register (formal, non-speculative — matching the nonprofit-style constraint already in the wiki conventions: 'comparable to a reference regulatory document'). Returns structured findings: {field, span, rule_id, severity, rationale, suggested_rewrite}. It never touches verbatim-locked or fixed template prose — those are out of its jurisdiction by construction.
proposals.value_to_whom: Physician: the last mile of voice quality inside field values ({{risks}}, {{purpose}}, {{procedures}}) where free text is unavoidable and where LLM mannerisms would otherwise leak through. Patient: consent field prose that actually reads at 8th grade in second person. Reviewer trust: findings are span-anchored and rule-cited, so a human can adjudicate them in seconds.
proposals.build_sketch: engine/ossicro/voice_check.py using the Anthropic SDK: client.messages.parse (model claude-opus-4-8, thinking adaptive, structured output schema for findings; prompt caching on the rubric+banned-list system prompt since it is stable across documents). Rubrics live in registry/voice-rubrics.json keyed to the template category field that already exists in template frontmatter (consent, fda-forms, ...). Findings write into the completeness ledger with status 'voice-flagged'. Calibration: the agent proposes severity; only the deterministic layer or a human can hard-fail a document — the LLM judge alone fails toward the human gate, consistent with the wiki's interpretive-rules principle.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Checking agent (LLM-as-judge over field values, structured findings output, cached rubric system prompt)

proposals.title: Flag-and-rewrite loop: slop is repaired before a human ever sees the draft
proposals.what_it_is: A bounded repair loop that runs after generation and before the human-review gate: deterministic lint + voice agent flag findings → a rewrite agent produces corrected text for the flagged spans only (it receives the span, the finding, the field's citation and rubric — not license to rewrite the document) → the deterministic lint, verbatim-hash check, and template-diff invariant re-run on the result → loop until clean or max 3 iterations, then surface any residual findings to the human alongside the draft. Every rewrite is recorded as a provenance event (original text, finding, replacement, agent, timestamp) so the audit trail shows what the AI changed and why — aligned with the Part 11 posture the wiki already claims.
proposals.value_to_whom: Physician: what lands in their review queue already reads as professional regulatory work product; their time goes to substance (are the risks right?) not copy-editing AI tics. This is the 'obscene amounts of paperwork, done correctly and framed correctly' promise made operational: volume with a floor on voice quality. Micro-CRO: the redline + provenance record is itself review evidence.
proposals.build_sketch: engine/ossicro/rewrite.py: orchestrate lint → voice_check → rewrite (Claude SDK, claude-opus-4-8; input is only the flagged spans with their findings, rubric excerpts, and field citations; structured output = replacement text per span) → re-validate. Hard rules: rewrites are span-scoped (engine splices them, model never returns whole documents); verbatim-locked and fixed-template text can never be a rewrite target; a span that fails 3 times is presented to the human with both versions and the unresolved finding. Non-delegable gates untouched — the loop ends at the same recorded human sign-off gates.json already enforces; it just guarantees the human reviews a clean candidate.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Rewriting agent (span-scoped repair) downstream of the checking agent; deterministic re-checks sandwich every LLM step

proposals.title: Consent readability and exculpatory-language gate (voice and correctness fused, patient-facing)
proposals.what_it_is: A specialized deterministic gate for consent-category documents: (a) grade-level scoring (Flesch-Kincaid + SMOG) on each consent field value against the template's stated 8th-grade target, (b) second-person compliance check, (c) an exculpatory-language detector — a curated phrase/pattern list of waiver and release constructions ('you waive', 'you release', 'the sponsor shall not be liable', 'you give up the right to') that violate 21 CFR 50.20, and (d) a benefits-section payment detector (payment listed as benefit violates the template's own 50.25(a)(3) instruction). Findings route to the rewrite loop; exculpatory hits are severity-critical and always surface to the human even after rewrite.
proposals.value_to_whom: Patient: the consent form — the one document a patient actually reads — is guaranteed legible and free of language that quietly strips their rights. Physician/IRB: exculpatory language and reading level are among the most common IRB stipulation causes; catching them pre-submission removes a full IRB round-trip, which directly serves the rapid-access timeline.
proposals.build_sketch: Extend style.py with a consent-specific rule pack in registry/style-rules.json (scope: category=consent). Readability via a small pure-Python scorer (no dependency risk); exculpatory patterns seeded from FDA's informed-consent guidance examples and the 50.20 text, each rule cited and status-classified. Wire as a named check that the informed-consent gate in gates.json requires to be green (or human-overridden with recorded rationale) before the ICF can move to the IRB-package assembly step. The voice agent's consent rubric (proposal 3) covers the judgment cases the pattern list can't (tone that minimizes risk without banned phrases).
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: n/a for the gate itself; feeds findings to the proposal-3/4 agents for repair

proposals.title: Golden-corpus regression harness for the anti-slop stack (false-positive control)
proposals.what_it_is: A fixtures-based eval in engine/tests: (a) a golden set of genuine regulatory prose — FDA model consent language, the 1572 commitments, M11 exemplar sections, passages from the sources/ library — that must pass the linter and voice agent with zero findings; (b) a slop set of the same passages deliberately degraded with LLM mannerisms (hedging, inflated transitions, promotional tone, hollow summaries) that must be flagged; (c) measured precision/recall per rule, run in CI on every change to style-rules.json, voice rubrics, or model version. Model upgrades to the checking agent get gated on this harness before deployment.
proposals.value_to_whom: The project itself, and by extension every persona: the fatal failure mode of an anti-slop system is flagging legitimate regulatory boilerplate as slop — that erodes physician trust faster than missed slop does, because regulatory register legitimately uses constructions ('shall', formal passives, defined-term repetition) that naive slop lists punish. This harness is what lets the banned-construction registry evolve without regressions, and what makes 'the paperwork reads professionally' a tested claim instead of an asserted one — the evidence-based-validation discipline applied to voice.
proposals.build_sketch: engine/tests/test_voice.py + engine/fixtures/voice/{golden,slop}/ with paired files. Golden passages sourced from documents already in sources/ (provenance noted per file). Slop variants generated once by a Claude SDK degradation script (prompt: inject specified mannerism classes, keep meaning) then frozen as fixtures — generation is one-time tooling, the fixtures are static. CI target: 0 findings on golden (hard), ≥95% of injected mannerisms flagged on slop set (tracked, threshold tunable). Report per-rule precision so weak rules get fixed or demoted to advisory rather than silently eroding trust.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: One-time fixture-degradation script (drafting agent role); the harness itself is deterministic pytest

key_insight: Voice quality becomes guaranteeable the moment it stops being a property of generation and becomes a property of architecture: because OSSICRO's templates already separate human-vetted regulatory prose from typed {{placeholders}} with per-field citations, the engine can structurally confine the LLM to filling blanks (diff-enforced, with verbatim-required passages hash-locked), which shrinks the anti-slop problem from 'judge whole AI-written documents' — where LLM judges are unreliable — to 'lint and judge short field values against per-field register rubrics,' where a cheap deterministic banned-construction layer plus a span-scoped Claude checking/rewrite loop can actually deliver the non-negotiable: every draft a physician sees already reads as professional regulatory work product, with the repair trail recorded for Part 11 and the human gates untouched.

On the rapid-access critical path, physician-controlled time is a small minority of calendar time — the long poles are counterparty clocks (the sponsor's yes under 312.53(a), the manufacturer's supply decision, IRB action, FDA's 30-day windows) that no document set can compress. OSSICRO's document layer already collapses drafting time; what makes heroic access ROUTINE is a case-level orchestration layer that (a) drives every physician-side interval to near-zero via intake-once reusable artifacts, and (b) runs Pathways A/B/C as parallel warm tracks from a single intake, so a counterparty's no costs days instead of restarting months. The wiki already states the doctrine ('keep pathway C warm', 'the one cycle-time lever the physician side actually controls' is feasibility-questionnaire turnaround); nothing in engine/ yet operationalizes it — the engine generates documents per-route, but there is no case state machine, no counterparty clock tracker, no automatic fallback flip. That conductor is the single most valuable capability to build next.

proposals.title: Rapid-Access Conductor — parallel-pathway case orchestrator
proposals.what_it_is: A per-patient case state machine that, from ONE structured intake (de-identified clinical picture + candidate drug + physician profile), instantiates all viable routes as parallel workstreams: Route 2 (one-patient site) as primary when a trial is open, Route 1 (individual-patient expanded access, 312.310) always held submission-ready as the warm hedge, Route 3 (sponsor-investigator IND) as the research fallback. It maintains a live 'days-to-drug' estimate per route, tracks every counterparty clock (sponsor response latency, manufacturer LOA, IRB cadence, FDA 30-day windows), and on any counterparty decline executes the fallback flip — the pre-assembled next-route package surfaces for physician review the same day. This is the wiki's Step 2.5 doctrine ('OSSICRO does not idle on the sponsor's clock') turned into running code.
proposals.value_to_whom: HCP/physician: converts a months-long serial gauntlet into one intake plus gate reviews; a sponsor's no costs days, not a restart. Patient: the expanded-access hedge means the fastest compliant route is always days from submission. Micro-CRO: the case ledger is its audit trail and workload queue.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Orchestrating agent (Claude Agent SDK) owns the case ledger: it interprets counterparty responses, decides which route-package to refresh, drafts next-action artifacts (chase emails, status narratives), and dispatches the existing generate/check/validate engine per route. Sub-agents: extraction agent (structures intake data once for all routes), drafting agents per route, checking agent that verifies cross-route consistency of shared facts (diagnosis, prior therapies, physician credentials).
proposals.build_sketch: New engine/ossicro/case.py: a Case model (patient-case id, elected + warm routes, per-route document-set status from the existing completeness ledger, counterparty-clock table with regulatory citations: 312.310(d) 30-day/emergency, IND 30-day, 15-working-day emergency written follow-up). SDK orchestrator wraps the existing cli.py generate/check/validate calls per route; registry/gates.json gates enforced unchanged — route ELECTION is a logged human decision, every submission/signature stays red-gated to the physician. Fallback flip is a proposal the physician approves, never an autonomous submission. Frontend: one case timeline showing the parallel tracks and whose clock each stalled day belongs to. Validate against the guiding-scenario as the golden path plus a 'sponsor declines at week 6' fixture proving the C-package is green in the ledger at flip time.

proposals.title: Regulatory Passport — once-per-physician verifiable site-qualification dossier
proposals.what_it_is: A standing, versioned, reusable artifact per physician: CV, medical license(s), GCP training certs, lab certifications/normal ranges, facility description, delegation-log skeleton, financial-disclosure baseline — ingested once, structured, citation-mapped, and kept current (expiry tracking on licenses/certs). From it, OSSICRO auto-populates on demand, for ANY sponsor or route: Form 1572, Forms 3454/3455, any sponsor's feasibility questionnaire, and the verifiable-site-qualification-dossier the wiki identifies as 'the single most persuasive artifact to a skeptical sponsor'. Turns the one cycle-time lever the physician actually controls — feasibility turnaround — from weeks into same-day.
proposals.value_to_whom: HCP: onboards once, answers every sponsor in days; the 'no track record' liability becomes a citation-complete dossier. Pharma: receives an internally consistent, verifiable package in the shape its site-qualification review expects, making the single-patient yes cheap to grant. Micro-CRO: tier-2 human review has a stable substrate to attest against.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Extraction agent parses uploaded CVs/licenses/certs into the structured schema with per-field provenance; drafting agent maps the passport onto arbitrary sponsor feasibility questionnaires (the genuinely hard NL task — sponsor forms are heterogeneous); checking agent verifies internal consistency (dates, names, addresses match across every generated artifact) and register (professional regulatory voice, zero LLM mannerisms) before anything reaches the physician.
proposals.build_sketch: New templates/site-management/physician-passport schema + engine/ossicro support: passport.json per physician with provenance per field (source document, page, extraction confidence — amber-flag anything below threshold for human confirmation). Generation targets already exist (form-fda-1572, 3454/3455 templates); add a questionnaire-mapper that takes an arbitrary sponsor questionnaire (PDF/doc) and emits a draft response with per-answer provenance back to the passport. Hard line preserved: the physician reviews and signs every instantiation; the 1572 and financial-disclosure attestations are gate-enforced (registry/gates.json); the passport never auto-transmits. Expiry watcher flags stale credentials before they poison a package.

proposals.title: Expanded-Access Fast Lane — submission-ready-in-24-hours Form 3926 kit
proposals.what_it_is: The end-to-end Pathway C package as a single one-day flow: Form FDA 3926 draft, treatment plan, manufacturer LOA-request letter, IRB concurrence request pre-shaped for chair/designated-member review, informed-consent document draft, drug-accountability record skeleton — plus the EMERGENCY variant: a telephone-authorization briefing sheet (what the physician says to FDA on the call) and an automatic 15-working-day tracker for the written follow-up under 312.310(d). Since the non-emergency 30-day clock starts at FDA RECEIPT, days-to-submission is the only interval the physician controls — this drives it to one.
proposals.value_to_whom: Patient: the fastest lawful route to drug is never the bottleneck on the physician's side. HCP: the pathway most likely to be needed urgently is the one with the least tolerance for paperwork fumbling — this makes it a form-fill plus three human acts (file, consent conversation, IRB submission). Also the Conductor's warm-hedge payload.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agent composes the treatment plan and LOA-request letter from intake data in regulatory register; checking agent validates the 3926 package against 312.305 criteria (serious condition, no comparable alternative, benefit justifies risk, no interference with development) and flags each criterion as met/needs-physician-judgment; a second-pass register agent strips any AI mannerism before the physician sees it.
proposals.build_sketch: Templates already exist (templates/expanded-access/: 3926, treatment plan, LOA). Build the FLOW: a registry-defined package 'expanded-access-individual' with a completeness ledger tuned to the 312.310 minimum, the emergency-use branch (phone-auth briefing + 15-working-day clock wired into the safety-clock pattern), and the IRB single-member-review cover letter. Gates unchanged and explicit: OSSICRO never files with FDA, never contacts the manufacturer autonomously, never conducts consent; the manufacturer's supply decision and IRB concurrence render as red/amber ledger items that only external humans can turn green. Acceptance test: synthetic patient fixture goes intake-to-green-ledger in under 24h of wall-clock with only the physician's gate actions pending.

proposals.title: Sponsor Engagement Kit + counterparty-latency tracker
proposals.what_it_is: The package and process for Step 2.5, where most one-patient site requests die. Contents: (1) the medical-affairs inquiry letter (drafted by OSSICRO, sent by a named human); (2) CDA readiness checklist; (3) the pre-populated feasibility-questionnaire response (from the Regulatory Passport); (4) the 'minimal-but-compliant one-patient site' acceptance memo written in the PHARMA reviewer's frame — what the sponsor's clin-ops/legal needs to see to make yes cheap (FMV-benchmarked budget, safe-harbor CTA structure, safety-data-exchange terms, the verifiable dossier); (5) a parallel manufacturer LOA request feeding the warm C-track. The tracker logs every counterparty exchange, computes latency, and triggers the Conductor's fallback evaluation when the sponsor stalls past a physician-set threshold.
proposals.value_to_whom: HCP: the outreach that used to require knowing-somebody-in-medical-affairs becomes a professional, complete first contact. Pharma: receives exactly the package its own review machinery needs — the wiki's [[single-patient-site-and-pharma-acceptance]] thesis operationalized. Patient: sponsor stall no longer silently burns weeks because the tracker makes the stall visible and the hedge ready.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agent produces the inquiry letter and acceptance memo in professional register; a distinct ADVERSARIAL checking agent red-teams the package from the sponsor reviewer's chair ('what would make me decline this?') before it goes out — a matching/simulation role that raises first-pass acceptance odds without ever claiming to predict the decision.
proposals.build_sketch: New templates/site-management entries (inquiry letter, acceptance memo, safety-data-exchange terms) registered in documents.json with citation manifests (312.53(a) sponsor discretion stated honestly in the memo itself). Tracker is a thin log in the Case model (proposal 1): timestamped exchanges, computed latency, threshold alert. Hard lines: every outbound communication is sent by a named human under their own name (Constitution-grade no-send rule mirrors OSSICRO's); the memo never asserts the sponsor must accept; the CTA/budget remain drafts for bilateral legal negotiation. Ship after proposals 1-2 since it consumes the passport and feeds the conductor.

proposals.title: Days-to-Drug clock engine and case dashboard
proposals.what_it_is: A deterministic critical-path model per route rendering one number the physician and patient can see: estimated days to drug in hand, decomposed into physician-side intervals (target ~0 via proposals 2-3), regulatory clocks (statutory, cited: 312.310(d) 30-day and emergency, IND 30-day under 312.40, 15-working-day emergency follow-up), and counterparty intervals (observed, never fabricated — shown as 'sponsor's clock, day 12' rather than a made-up estimate where no validated statistic exists, per the wiki's own honesty rule about refusal rates). Wired to the completeness ledger so every red/amber item shows its exact resolving question and whose action it needs.
proposals.value_to_whom: Patient + HCP: the single most anxiety-reducing artifact in the system — where the case is stuck and whose move it is. Micro-CRO: portfolio view of every open case's stalled clock is its daily work queue. Also the measurement layer that proves 'routine' is being achieved (median physician-side days across cases).
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Mostly deterministic (n/a for the clock math itself); a narrative agent drafts the weekly plain-language case-status summary for the physician and a patient-appropriate version, checked for register and for containing no probability claims not derived from statutory clocks.
proposals.build_sketch: engine/ossicro/clocks.py: per-route DAG of intervals with type tags {physician, statutory (with CFR citation), counterparty-observed}; feeds off the Case model and completeness ledger. Frontend panel on the existing frontend: route lanes, current blocking item, whose clock. Explicit anti-fabrication rule in code: counterparty intervals display observed elapsed time only, no predicted durations without a cited basis. No gates touched — this is pure observability; it changes no document and sends nothing.

proposals.title: One-Visit Feasibility Verdict (HCP adoption trigger)
proposals.what_it_is: A single session where the physician enters the patient's clinical picture and the target drug, and gets back in minutes: (1) the Mode A / Mode B / expanded-access triage with reasons; (2) the four hard-stop checks that today kill projects late — central-IRB access + fee estimate, research-liability insurance gap, NCD 310.1 billing-qualification pre-screen, and site-readiness scoring against the protocol's schedule of assessments; (3) the honest hours/cost table for the chosen route (the wiki already commits to under-promising: ~25-40h Mode A, ~80-150h Mode B, ~8-15h EA); and (4) the complete generated draft dossier for that route. The persona page is explicit that a physician who discovers the insurance or billing gap after assembling the package 'has been failed by the tool' — this makes the gaps the FIRST output, not a footnote.
proposals.value_to_whom: HCP/physician — this is what makes them say yes. The intimidation-points list (hcp-physician.md barriers 1-8) shows the physician's real blocker is not any single document but not knowing the total burden and the hidden hard stops before committing. A physician will adopt a tool that tells them in one sitting 'here is the route, here is what it will actually cost you in hours and dollars, here are the two things software cannot fix, and here is the entire draft package if you proceed.' Secondary value to patient (faster, honest go/no-go) and pharma (conduct-infeasible sites screen out before they ever apply).
proposals.build_sketch: Compose existing pieces: three-pathways-triage logic + completeness-ledger + the site-readiness checklist generator (already specified to derive from the schedule of assessments) into one orchestrated flow on the existing generate/check/validate engine. Add a small curated dataset: current central-IRB fee schedules, known research-rider insurance products, and the NCD 310.1 qualifying-trial worksheet. Output is a triage report (drafts + explicit red/amber items resolving to the physician) — no gate is touched; the physician still signs, contracts the IRB, and buys the insurance. The verdict document itself states which items are interpretive market estimates per the wiki's own convention.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Orchestrating agent runs triage Q&A and drives drafting agents for the route-specific package; an extraction agent parses the protocol/IB to derive the schedule-of-assessments readiness checklist; a checking agent verifies every red-flag item (insurance, billing, IRB) is answered or explicitly open before the verdict renders.

proposals.title: Expanded-Access Fast Path as the wedge product
proposals.what_it_is: A purpose-built end-to-end flow for single-patient expanded access (21 CFR 312.310): draft Form 3926, the manufacturer letter-of-authorization request, the IRB concurrence package on the selected central IRB's own forms, and the Part 50 consent document — targeting the physician's total time at or below the wiki's own 8-15h estimate, with the FDA submission and every signature remaining explicit human acts. This is the smallest complete instance of the rapid-access story: one physician, one patient, one drug, days not months.
proposals.value_to_whom: Patient (fastest lawful route to the medication — EA is treatment, not research, so it is the purest rapid-access case) and HCP (lowest-commitment first experience of OSSICRO: no IND, no 30-day clock, no standing sponsor obligations). It is also the natural first paid touchpoint for the Micro-CRO's Tier 2 review. Adoption logic: a physician who succeeds once on the 8-15h EA path is the physician who later attempts Mode B.
proposals.build_sketch: Form 3926 has a fixed FDA schema like the 1572 the plan already targets in Phase 2 — extend the same generate/check/validate pattern. Add the LOA-request letter template (professional register, addressed to the manufacturer's Medical Affairs EA contact), the expedited single-patient IRB package, and the EA-specific ICF with the treatment-not-research framing. Gates preserved: physician signs 3926, manufacturer decides supply (non-delegable counterparty judgment), IRB concurs, physician conducts consent. Ship it as the first full vertical slice ahead of the full Mode B engine.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agents populate 3926/LOA/ICF from the structured clinical picture; a checking agent validates against the Subpart I requirements and the completeness ledger; a professional-voice validation agent enforces regulatory register on the LOA letter (the correctness + no-AI-slop requirement made executable).

proposals.title: IND-Maintenance Copilot (the standing-job reducer)
proposals.what_it_is: For Mode B physicians post-approval: a maintenance layer that drives the safety-clock engine (7/15-day computation and escalation, never auto-filing), drafts IND annual reports/DSURs from accumulated study data, drafts protocol amendments in the six-amendment-type state machine, tracks IRB continuing-review dates, and packages each into single-pass review sessions. Target: cut the wiki's estimated 10-20 h/month IND-maintenance burden to the irreducible judgment core (causality calls, sign-offs) — the wiki itself says the judgment floor is real; this removes everything above it.
proposals.value_to_whom: HCP/sponsor-investigator — the wiki names IND maintenance 'a standing part-time job' and it is the reason physicians who could start a Mode B study still won't. Making year two as tractable as day one is what converts OSSICRO from a startup tool into an ongoing practice tool. Secondary: pharma and FDA counterparties get on-time, consistent filings from small sites; the Micro-CRO gets a recurring Tier 2 revenue surface.
proposals.build_sketch: Phase 3/4 extension: wire the existing safety-clock engine and milestone tracker to drafting agents for 312.33 annual reports and 312.30 amendments; every clock that reaches an accountable act fails to a named human gate (causality/expectedness is flagged qualified-physician-only per hand-off 9 of the perspective matrix). Drafts carry span-level provenance into the single-pass-review UX so the monthly review session triages to judgment calls only.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agents assemble annual reports/amendments from structured events; checking agents run cross-document consistency (protocol vs ICF vs IB versions) before human review; a coordination agent sequences IRB continuing-review and FDA anniversaries — no agent ever files.

proposals.title: Patient Story-to-Clinical-Picture intake agent
