proposals.what_it_is: The patient-side front door: a conversational intake that lets a patient or caregiver tell their story in plain language (diagnosis, treatments failed, current status, records upload) and converts it into the structured, de-identified clinical profile the matching engine consumes — delivered to the patient's physician for one-pass validation, never acted on directly. This makes 'a physician OR patient inputs information' real while preserving the design gate that raw match output goes to the HCP.
proposals.value_to_whom: Patient — today the entry question 'is there a trial for my condition?' requires either a physician with time or unguided ClinicalTrials.gov self-search. This gives them a genuinely useful action they can complete alone, with the output landing where it is clinically actionable. HCP — receives a pre-structured, matching-ready profile instead of doing intake labor, which is what makes physicians willing to receive patient-initiated requests at all.
proposals.build_sketch: Claude Agent SDK conversational agent over a fixed clinical-profile schema (condition codes, prior lines, key labs/genomics, performance status), running inside the privacy state machine: de-identified profile only, no PHI egress pre-authorization, matching under the 164.512(i) preparatory-review frame once the physician holds it. Physician validates/corrects the profile before matching output is generated. Portal copy comes from the IRB-reviewable status-language library (no 'a trial is available for you' phrasing, per the patient page's expectation-management rules).
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Conversational extraction agent (narrative → structured profile with confidence flags); a checking agent marks fields as patient-reported vs record-confirmed so the physician's validation pass is triaged; the matching agent then consumes the validated profile.

proposals.title: Plain-language status portal generated from the completeness ledger
proposals.what_it_is: The patient-facing transparency floor made automatic: portal status ('your doctor received N candidate programs on [date]', expected-response windows, plain-language explanations of why candidates were or were not pursued, and the designed 'no suitable option found' outcome with standard-of-care framing) generated directly from ledger and milestone state — with all patient-visible copy drawn from a pre-approved, IRB-reviewable language library rather than free generation.
proposals.value_to_whom: Patient — the patient page commits to 'never left watching a spinner' with stated recourse; this is what makes the paternalistic HCP-mediation gate tolerable and keeps seriously ill people engaged instead of abandoned. HCP — the expected-response window and auto-drafted explanations discharge a communication duty they otherwise carry manually.
proposals.build_sketch: Deterministic mapping from ledger/milestone states to a versioned library of pre-written plain-language status strings (treated as recruitment-adjacent, IRB-reviewed material per the wiki's own position). A drafting agent proposes the on-request 'why/why-not' summaries from the match rationale; the physician approves before release. Hard rule encoded: no status string may imply enrollment likelihood pre-IRB-approval.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agent for the on-request rationale summaries; a claims-checking agent lints every patient-visible string against the forbidden-implications ruleset (a small, testable validator).

proposals.title: Consent-comprehension package (document side only)
proposals.what_it_is: For each study, auto-drafted patient-tailored consent supports: the key-information summary at the patient's reading level, a mandatory Mode B research-is-not-treatment section (already a drafting requirement on the patient page), a translated version where needed, and a 'questions you may want to ask' preparation sheet keyed to the eight 50.25(a) elements — including a plain statement of the injury-compensation answer, which the completeness ledger already treats as a blocking Mode B item. The consent conversation itself remains untouched and human.
proposals.value_to_whom: Patient — comprehension in 'language understandable to them' (50.20) is their statutory right and the place n-of-1 studies are ethically weakest (therapeutic misconception, dual-role conflict). HCP/IRB — a Mode B submission that arrives with the independent-discussant default, the dual-role disclosure, and honest 50.25(a)(6) language pre-drafted is materially easier to approve; the patient page predicts IRBs scrutinize exactly these items hardest.
proposals.build_sketch: Extend the existing ICF templates with a comprehension layer: reading-level targeting, the mandatory Mode B distinguishing language, and the question-prep sheet generated from the study's actual risk/alternative/compensation content. All outputs are IRB-submission components (drafts for the board's judgment), versioned with the ICF. No eConsent execution — document mechanics only, per informed-consent-document-vs-event.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agent produces the tailored summary and question sheet from the protocol + ICF; a checking agent verifies all eight 50.25(a) elements and the 50.20 no-exculpatory-language rule; a readability validator enforces the target level.

proposals.title: Micro-CRO-in-a-Box: quality system, SOPs, and inspection readiness
proposals.what_it_is: Generation and maintenance of the thin entity's entire operating substrate: the minimum SOP set the micro-cro page enumerates (document control, TORO intake/change control, safety intake with clock discipline, monitoring, deviation/CAPA, training records, vendor oversight, retention, Part 11 system validation), training-record scaffolding for every staffed role, and a recurring BIMO-readiness self-audit that walks the entity's records the way an FDA inspector would and files findings as CAPA drafts.
proposals.value_to_whom: Micro-CRO — 312.52(b) enforcement parity means the thin entity must run like a real CRO; the fixed cost of standing that up is the barrier to the entity existing at all, and OSSICRO's whole legal architecture depends on it existing. Indirectly every other persona: no accountable entity, no Tier 3, no lawful home for transferred obligations.
proposals.build_sketch: Phase 6 accelerant: SOP templates drafted from the wiki's own enumerated minimum set, instantiated to the entity's actual staffing and assumed-obligation scope; the self-audit agent runs read-only over the entity's document store against the essential-records matrix and the SOPs' own requirements, outputting an inspection-readiness report + draft CAPAs for human disposition. Nothing self-executes; the quality manager reviews and signs.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agents for SOPs/CAPAs; an auditing/checking agent for the periodic mock-BIMO walk (records vs requirements, gap list with citations).

proposals.title: TORO builder with obligation-by-obligation scoping and oversight-evidence checklists
proposals.what_it_is: A structured instrument builder for 21 CFR 312.52 transfers: pick obligations from the CFR-anchored assumable set (the micro-cro page's table), and the tool drafts the TORO with each obligation described specifically, auto-flags that anything undescribed is retained by the sponsor-investigator (silence = retained, stated in the instrument), blocks judgment-core transfers (e.g., 312.32 causality) unless a qualified medical monitor is named for that study, and emits the matching oversight-evidence checklist both parties must maintain under E6(R3).
proposals.value_to_whom: Micro-CRO — the TORO is its entire legal perimeter; a mis-scoped transfer is enforcement exposure at sponsor parity. HCP/sponsor-investigator — sees exactly what they still own after the transfer, which is the thing 312.52's silence rule makes dangerous to get wrong. Pharma — a clean, specific TORO is part of what makes the small-site configuration acceptable.
proposals.build_sketch: Encode the assumable-obligation table as a schema (obligation → CFR anchor → required staffing/qualification → oversight evidence). Drafting agent instantiates the instrument; a validation rule refuses to include any obligation whose staffing prerequisite (named qualified human) is unmet — the tool can decline to draft an unlawful scope while never itself being the transferee. Counsel review and both signatures remain human gates.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Drafting agent for the instrument text; checking agent for scope-vs-staffing consistency and the enumeration-specificity requirement of 312.52(a).

proposals.title: Tier-2 Review Workbench (micro-CRO unit economics)
proposals.what_it_is: The paid-human-review console: for each incoming study package, a single-pass review session where every span is triaged by provenance class (deterministic/template vs inferred/interpretive), cross-document inconsistencies are pre-flagged by the adversarial pre-review pass, and the reviewer's dispositions, credentials, and sign-offs are captured into the Part 11 audit trail. Goal: one qualified reviewer clears multiples of today's throughput at equal or better quality, because their attention lands only on judgment calls.
proposals.value_to_whom: Micro-CRO — Tier 2 is its main revenue surface and reviewer-hours are its cost; this is the difference between a viable $90/hr-escalation business (the MICHR precedent the plan cites) and one that can't scale past a founder. HCP — cheaper, faster accountable review of their packages. Pharma — a legible record of exactly which qualified human reviewed what, which is the trust product itself.
proposals.build_sketch: Build the already-adopted strategist items 3+4 (provenance-linked drafts, attention-triaged review UX, adversarial pre-review) into a reviewer-facing product with review-session records: reviewer identity/qualifications, spans reviewed, changes ordered, e-signature per Part 11. The reviewer's professional judgment is the product; the workbench only concentrates it.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Adversarial checking agents run pre-review (consistency, completeness, citation verification) and rank findings; drafting agents apply the reviewer's ordered changes for re-review — the human disposes of every finding.

proposals.title: Sponsor-side dossier verifier (free pharma tool) + qualification dossier
proposals.what_it_is: The verifiable site-qualification dossier (hash-chained, requirement → artifact → citation → signer) paired with what makes it land: a free, standalone, no-install verification tool a pharma feasibility reviewer runs against the received dossier to confirm integrity, completeness against the activation checklist (1572, CVs/licenses, Part 54 disclosures, training, lab certs, delegation log, storage SOPs), internal consistency, and open items — turning 'trust this new site' into 'verify this dossier in twenty minutes.'
proposals.value_to_whom: Pharma — its trust gate is entity-shaped and evidence-shaped; the pharma page says the persuasive artifact is a complete, internally consistent, verifiable dossier, and a zero-cost verification path is what makes a reviewer actually engage with an unknown one-physician site instead of presumptively rejecting it. HCP — the no-track-record barrier (feasibility screen barrier #6) becomes surmountable. Micro-CRO — the dossier is explicitly its durable asset and moat.
proposals.build_sketch: The dossier format already exists in the design (adopted improvement 6). Add: (1) a deterministic verifier CLI/web page (hash-chain check, checklist coverage, signer identity, ledger-declared open items) that pharma runs without trusting OSSICRO's servers; (2) a human-readable reviewer summary mapped to the standard feasibility-questionnaire and SQV categories. The supply/acceptance decision remains pharma's non-delegable counterparty judgment — the tool only collapses their verification cost.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: n/a for the verifier core (deterministic by design — that is the point); a drafting agent produces the reviewer-facing narrative summary from the verified manifest.

proposals.title: Pharma-shaped submission export: IIS portals, EA intake, and the safety-data-exchange draft
proposals.what_it_is: An export layer that emits the physician's package in the receiving company's own expected shape: the IIS concept/full-proposal on the target company's portal structure (Medical Affairs conventions, FMV-framed budget draft, Sunshine-aware), the expanded-access request per that manufacturer's published §561A policy, and a draft safety-data-exchange agreement so ICSRs flow both directions on contractual timelines — the item the pharma page names as a hard requirement OSSICRO must arrange, plus reconciliation tracking once live. Includes a maintained, structured index of manufacturers' public expanded-access policies and IIS program requirements.
proposals.value_to_whom: Pharma — requests that arrive in the shape its Medical Affairs machinery expects, respecting the MA/CD firewall, are the ones that get reviewed rather than dropped; the SDEA draft removes a named blocker to accepting a sponsor-investigator counterparty. HCP/patient — this is the 'hooks and access into the pharmaceutical layer' from the vision: the difference between a complete package and a complete package the counterparty can actually process.
proposals.build_sketch: Build per-company submission profiles (schema of required fields/sections per public IIS portal and EA policy — Genentech's program is the cited model), starting with the top oncology/rare-disease manufacturers; a mapping layer renders OSSICRO's canonical artifacts into each profile. SDEA template drafted from the safety-management-plan spine with the study's actual reporting timelines. All submissions are human-authorized sends; routing is Medical-Affairs-only by construction, never Commercial.
proposals.uses_claude_sdk: Extraction agents parse and keep current each company's public IIS/EA requirements into the profile schema (living-compliance pattern); drafting agents render the mapped submission and the SDEA draft; a checking agent validates field coverage against the target profile before human review.

key_insight: Each persona's "yes" is gated by a different scarce currency, and none of them is "more documents": the physician needs the total burden and the two software-unfixable gaps (insurance, billing) disclosed in one sitting before committing; the patient needs a mediated but never-opaque status channel and a fast lawful treatment route (expanded access is the purest rapid-access wedge — 8-15h, treatment not research); the micro-CRO needs its fixed quality-system cost collapsed so the accountable entity can exist at all; and pharma needs verification to be cheaper than rejection — a dossier its reviewer can machine-verify plus submissions arriving in its own Medical-Affairs-shaped intake formats. The highest-leverage builds therefore convert OSSICRO's existing drafting engine into those four currencies (a one-visit feasibility verdict, an EA fast path, quality-system-in-a-box, and a sponsor-side verifier + pharma-shaped export) rather than widening the template library — with every accountable act still terminating in a qualified human per the non-delegable gate list.
